chunks.text: Satish Rao is a Professor in EECS at UC Berkeley. Contact information includes 687 Soda Hall, phone 510-642-4328, and email satishr@cs.berkeley.edu. Research areas include Biosystems & Computational Biology (BIO) and Theory (THY). Research centers include Center for the Theoretical Foundations of Learning, Inference, Information, Intelligence, Mathematics and Microeconomics at Berkeley (CLIMB) and Simons Institute for the Theory of Computing (SITC).
chunks.question: What are Satish Rao's research areas and contact details?

chunks.text: Selected publications include: 'A tight bound on approximating arbitrary metrics by tree metrics' (2003), 'Distributed object location in a dynamic network' (2002), 'Divide-and-conquer approximation algorithms using spreading metrics' (2000), 'Multicommodity max-flow min-cut theorems and their use in designing approximation algorithms' (1999), 'Portable and efficient parallel computing using the BSP model' (1999), 'Small distortion and volume preserving embeddings for planar and Euclidean metrics' (1999), 'Beyond the flow decomposition barrier' (1998), '"Ratio regions": A technique for image segmentation' (1996), and 'Packet routing and job-shop scheduling in O(congestion + dilation) steps' (1994).
chunks.question: What are some of Satish Rao's key publications?
chunks.answer: Publications on metrics, algorithms, and computing models

chunks.text: Selected honors and awards include Association for Computing Machinery (ACM) Fellow (2013), Delbert Ray Fulkerson Prize (2012), and Okawa Research Grant (1999).
chunks.question: What awards has Satish Rao received?
chunks.answer: ACM Fellow, Fulkerson Prize, Okawa Grant
